description: The Trend Vision One Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server enables natural language interaction between your favourite AI tooling and the Trend Vision One web APIs.  This allows users to harness the power of Large Language Models (LLM) to interpret and respond to security events.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Tool requests broad permissions (exec/fs/network). Validate input parameters using Enums where possible, and restrict file system operations to explicit allowed directories.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Tool requests broad permissions (exec/fs/network). Validate input parameters using Enums where possible, and restrict file system operations to explicit allowed directories.

findings.title: DoS Resilience — Missing Rate Limit / Timeout
findings.description: tool performs network or execution operations but declares no rate-limit, timeout, or retry configuration
findings.recommendation: Declare explicit rate-limit, timeout, and retry configuration for all network and execution tools. Implement exponential back-off and surface resource state to the calling agent.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Tool requests broad permissions (exec/fs/network). Validate input parameters using Enums where possible, and restrict file system operations to explicit allowed directories.

findings.title: Scope Mismatch
findings.description: tool name "get_user_identity_by_subscription" implies read-only operation but declares exec permission
findings.recommendation: Ensure tool names, descriptions, and permission declarations are internally consistent. Use explicit naming conventions that fully reflect actual capabilities.

findings.title: DoS Resilience — Missing Rate Limit / Timeout
findings.description: tool performs network or execution operations but declares no rate-limit, timeout, or retry configuration
findings.recommendation: Declare explicit rate-limit, timeout, and retry configuration for all network and execution tools. Implement exponential back-off and surface resource state to the calling agent.

findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
findings.description: tool name or description implies arbitrary script/code execution (evaluate_script, execute javascript, etc.)
findings.recommendation: This tool can execute arbitrary code. If not strictly needed, remove it. If required, you MUST set approval_required: true in your MCP client config to ensure human-in-the-loop confirmation.

findings.title: DoS Resilience — Missing Rate Limit / Timeout
findings.description: tool performs network or execution operations but declares no rate-limit, timeout, or retry configuration
findings.recommendation: Declare explicit rate-limit, timeout, and retry configuration for all network and execution tools. Implement exponential back-off and surface resource state to the calling agent.

findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Tool requests broad permissions (exec/fs/network). Validate input parameters using Enums where possible, and restrict file system operations to explicit allowed directories.

findings.title: Insecure Secret Handling
findings.description: input parameter "token" appears to accept a secret or credential
findings.recommendation: Avoid accepting raw credentials as input parameters. Use secret managers (e.g. 1Password CLI, AWS Secrets Manager) and ensure credentials are never logged or stored in agent traces.
